news.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
news.summary: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

news.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
news.summary: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.

news.title: Google turns Chrome into an AI co-worker for the workplace
news.summary: Google brings Gemini-powered "auto browse" capabilities to Chrome for enterprise users, letting workers automate tasks like research, data entry, and more.

news.title: Threads is adding Live Chats to boost real-time engagement
news.summary: Users can send messages, photos, videos, links, and emoji reactions in Live Chats.

news.title: Google makes an interesting choice with its new agent-building tool for enterprises
news.summary: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform takes an interesting approach: It is geared for IT and technical users.

news.title: Watch Sony’s elite ping-pong robot beat top-ranked players

news.title: Google unveils two new TPUs designed for the "agentic era"
news.summary: Google's new generation of Tensor AI chips is actually two chips, one for inference and one for training.

news.summary: FBI suspects foreign spies may be targeting scientists with access to government secrets.

news.summary: The cyber capabilities of AI models have experts rattled. AI’s social skills may be just as dangerous.

news.title: New York Bans Government Employees from Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
news.summary: A new executive order seen by WIRED prohibits New York state employees from using insider knowledge to enrich themselves with prediction market bets.

news.title: AI Tools Are Helping Mediocre North Korean Hackers Steal Millions
news.summary: One group of hackers used AI for everything from vibe coding their malware to creating fake company websites—and stole as much as $12 million in three months.
